2. **`Store::resolve_agent_pubkey(slug, host)`** (`src/state/profiles.rs:56-70`) reverse-looks-up `relay_profiles` by slug+host. So **`resolve_agent_pubkey("haiku1", local_host)` already returns the ordinal-1 selected pubkey** with zero new code ‚Äî and that exact call is *already* step (4) of `resolve_recipient` (`chat_write.rs:309-318`).
 [...] | `idref.rs:112` `looks_like_codename` gate in `extract_mentions` | Accept any agent-slug-shaped token (`[A-Za-z0-9._-]`, optionally `@host`). Let `resolve_recipient` decide validity. This is the one real edit: the gate must stop filtering to NATO-codename shape. | [...] | `chat_write.rs:300-308` `find_session_by_codename` step | Remove. The bare-slug step (309-318) + `resolve_agent_pubkey` covers `@haiku`/`@haiku1`. |
| `chat_write.rs:333-348` `find_session_by_codename` fn | Delete (no other caller ‚Äî confirmed by grep). | [...] Assistant: Explorer A (targeting) is in ‚Äî and the key win: `resolve_agent_pubkey("haiku1", host)` *already* resolves the ordinal label to the selected pubkey (each instance publishes kind:0 under its display label, already stored in `relay_profiles.slug`). So `@haiku1` targeting is mostly **deletion** + relaxing one mention gate in `idref.rs`. Waiting on the last explorer (SessionId Display + display surfaces) before I synthesize. [...] - **Targeting** ‚Äî relax the mention gate in `idref.rs` to accept `@haiku`/`@haiku1`; delete `find_session_by_codename` + the "must name a concrete session codename" bail in `chat_write.rs`. `resolve_agent_pubkey("haiku1", host)` already resolves the label‚Üíselected-pubkey (each instance publishes kind:0 under its display label), so this is mostly deletion.
